item.title: Itausa, GIC and Equipav Said to Create Group to Bid for Copasa
item.description: Itausa SA, the Singapore sovereign wealth fund GIC and Equipav Saneamento SA are planning to jointly bid to become strategic investors in the privatization process for Copasa, the sanitation company owned by the government of Minas Gerais, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: BofA’s Hartnett Warns Mega-IPOs Risk Bubble Like Roaring ‘20s
item.description: Mega IPOs like those eyed by SpaceX and OpenAI threaten to push the weighting of tech in equity benchmarks beyond market-bubble levels of concentration, according to Bank of America Corp. strategist Michael Hartnett.

item.title: AI-Fueled Rally Puts S&P 500 on Track for Eighth Weekly Gain
item.description: US equity futures rose as investors struck an upbeat tone ahead of a long holiday weekend, with optimism fueled by hopes for resolution of hostilities in the Middle East, resilient economic data and relentless enthusiasm for artificial intelligence-linked trades.

item.title: Mexican Economy Contracted Less Than Expected in First Quarter
item.description: Mexico’s economy shrank less than expected in the first quarter, with activity dragged down by weakness in agriculture, manufacturing and services.

item.title: Canada Retail Sales Up 0.6% in April as Gas Lifts Receipts
item.description: Canadian retail sales continued to rise last month after a solid first quarter, but surging gasoline prices appear to be increasingly eating into household budgets.

item.title: Mexico Mid-May Inflation Slowed After Banxico Ended Easing Cycle
item.description: Mexico’s annual inflation slowed in line with expectations in early May, after the central bank late last month delivered a final interest rate cut of a two-year easing cycle.

item.title: Musk’s Latest Venture Gives Space Stocks Their Own Tesla Surge
item.description: With the highly-anticipated debut of SpaceX on the US stock market around the corner, euphoria for Elon Musk’s rocket venture is drawing comparisons to Tesla Inc.’s effect on electric-vehicle stocks.

item.title: Kayne Anderson Raises $5 Billion for Latest Real Estate Fund
item.description: Kayne Anderson Capital Advisors’ real estate arm has raised $5.12 billion for its largest-ever opportunistic fund, blowing past its original target of $3 billion amid booming demand for healthcare and consumer-related services.

item.title: Deutsche, JPMorgan See Weaker Canadian Dollar as Inflation Cools
item.description: Deutsche Bank and JPMorgan are piling up on wagers that the Canadian dollar will continue to fall in 2026 as the country’s tame inflation readings fuel a rethink of the Bank of Canada’s interest-rate hike outlook.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 22, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.2% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York, with stocks heading toward their longest streak of weekly gains since 2023.

item.title: Mercuria Gets October UK Trial in Multibillion Dollar Hormuz Spat
item.description: Commodity trader Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. was granted an early trial over an alleged distortion to the world’s benchmark oil tanker rate that it argues is costing the company hundreds of millions of dollars.

item.title: Only in Argentina: Junk-Rated Bonds Yield Less Than US Treasuries
item.description: For all of President Javier Milei’s libertarian reforms, there are still all sorts of byzantine financial rules in place, like restrictions on lending and moving money overseas, that are creating distortions in Argentina’s capital markets.

item.title: South Africa Proposes Rules to Entice Local EV Battery-Making
item.description: South Africa plans to change its auto-industry incentive program to channel more support to electric-vehicle battery manufacturers, boosting the nation’s prospects of becoming a manufacturing hub for the fast-growing technology.

item.title: Germany Confirms Decision to Buy 40% Stake in Tankmaker KNDS
item.description: The German government confirmed that it wants to acquire a 40% stake in KNDS NV for several billion euros before the tankmaker’s planned initial public offering this summer.

item.title: Investec Seeks Ireland Banking License as Lender Eyes EU Bounty
item.description: Investec has applied for a banking license in Ireland as it seeks to push into Europe to tap one of the world’s largest financial markets.

item.title: Bellwether Industrial Metal Copper Is Trading Like an AI Stock
item.description: Copper, famed for tracking shifts in the global industrial economy, is trading like a high-flying tech stock as investors bet that skyrocketing power use for artificial intelligence will fuel a surge in demand.

item.title: Big Oil’s Bumper Iran Profits Become a Juicy Tax Target for Europe
item.description: A number of countries are looking at ways to get extra revenue from energy companies.

item.title: Eutelsat Stock Takes Off as SpaceX IPO Triggers Satellite Rally
item.description: Shares in Eutelsat Communications are headed for their best week in nearly a year, as enthusiasm over the highly anticipated initial public offering of Elon Musk’s SpaceX spurs investor flows into the sector.

item.title: Central Banks Struggle to Keep Inflation Anchored as Bond Markets Bet on Rising Prices
item.description: The Iran war is causing price hikes and inflicting damage on a global economy that’s barely recovered from the last inflation spike.

item.title: Amazon’s Jassy Sees Company’s Breadth as a Strength
item.description: Few companies serve as many consumers and businesses

item.title: Britain’s Economy Turns Sour Just as Starmer Had a Reason to Brag
item.description: A smile on his face, Keir Starmer approached reporters outside Downing Street on Thursday to talk up recent data that suggested that Britain was the fastest growing economy in the Group of Seven.

item.title: Warsh to Be Sworn in as Fed Chief During White House Ceremony
item.description: Kevin Warsh, who’s promised the biggest shakeup in decades at the US central bank, is set to be sworn into office Friday in a White House ceremony as the 17th chair of the Federal Reserve.

item.title: Reeves’ Summer Discounts Give BOE a Helping Hand, Analysis Shows
item.description: Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves’ plans to help families cope with the cost of living this summer could also help the Bank of England, according to Bloomberg economists.

item.title: OHB and KNDS Weigh Tweaks to Share Sale Timetables to Avoid SpaceX
item.description: Two companies planning major share sales in Europe are considering pushing back the launches of their deals to avoid the expected market melee around SpaceX’s record initial public offering in the US.

item.title: India RBI to Pay Record Dividend, Misses Market Estimates
item.description: India’s central bank will transfer 2.87 trillion rupees ($30 billion) to the government, a record but one that fell short of expectations even as the Iran war pushes up energy prices and strains public finances.

item.title: This Wouldn’t Be Much of a Rally Without AI Stocks
item.description: Equal-weighted S&P 500 is unchanged since war broke out.

item.title: China’s AI-Made Video Is Changing the Entertainment Landscape
item.description: Such services pose an existential threat to traditional entertainment.

item.title: The $4.50 Gas Economy: Fewer Nights Out, More Belt-Tightening
item.description: Higher prices at the pump are making Avarisse Crawford rethink her spending. The 33-year-old has started scaling back her “fun budget,” meaning fewer steak dinners and happy hours with coworkers — instead she’s seeking out free activities, like going to the park.

item.title: Zara Owner Inditex’s CEO Bets on Diversification, AI for Growth
item.description: Zara owner Inditex SA is banking on diversification across brands and countries as well as artificial intelligence to spur growth at the world’s largest listed retailer, Chief Executive Officer Óscar García Maceiras said.

item.title: Buyout Bankers Go Big on Debt Pre-Sales to Thwart Fickle Markets
item.description: Timing can make or break a leveraged debt sale. Get it right, and banks secure cheap financing for their client and maximize fees. Get it wrong, and they can end up with their profits — and reputation — on the line.
